mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a healthy labor market, supporting consumer confidence and corporate earnings, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 6%, or the correlation between UNRATE and forward returns is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is at historically low levels, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy, encouraging borrowing and investment, which boosts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or average returns in this regime are not statistically different from periods with higher rates.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return in triggered periods is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and increasing housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer confidence and investment in real assets, which are strong tailwinds for broad economic activity and equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 45% of periods where both conditions are met, or the joint signal does not statistically outperform individual signals.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a negative real interest rate environment, which typically encourages investors to seek inflation hedges like equities, driving up valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points, or the correlation is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rapidly rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) during an economic downturn signals deteriorating corporate earnings prospects and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the average return in triggered periods is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic expansion, which supports corporate profitability and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO shows positive year-over-year growth, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and forward returns is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening construction activity and potentially broader economic slowdown, which negatively impacts future corporate earnings and equity prices, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the average return in triggered periods is not significantly negative.
